steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Kyrgyzstan's constitution, enacted in 2010 and ______ three of these features." The word enacted is a past participle modifying constitution. The blank must also modify constitution in parallel with enacted, so it needs a participle, not a full verb form.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — had contained: past perfect verb phrase — a full finite verb form, not a participle. It would need a subject to form a clause, which disrupts the noun phrase structure. ✗
  • Choice B — contained: simple past verb, not a present participle. It acts as a finite verb rather than a modifier parallel to enacted. ✗
  • Choice C — containing: present participle, which can modify a noun just like the past participle enacted. Both serve as adjectival modifiers of constitution, forming a grammatical parallel. ✓
  • Choice D — contains: third-person singular present verb — a finite verb form that breaks the parallel modifier structure. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
